issue.title: Bug: Blank-screen regression v2 — App.tsx:152 passes fresh {} to useStaleWorkDetection → infinite re-render
issue.body:
## Symptom

Same blank-screen / `Maximum update depth exceeded` symptom as #210, with KanbanBoard crashing the whole React tree. Surfaces specifically when no active project is selected (the "NONE no-id" path).

Console output during the crash:

```
The result of getSnapshot should be cached to avoid an infinite loop
mountSyncExternalStore @ react-dom-client.development.js:8129
[IssuesSection] activeProject changed: NONE no-id
[IssuesSection] fetchIssues called, activeProject: NONE
[IssuesSection] No active project - clearing issues
Uncaught Error: Maximum update depth exceeded.
    at forceStoreRerender (react-dom-client.development.js:8261:18)
    at updateStoreInstance (react-dom-client.development.js:8241:39)
    ...
An error occurred in the <KanbanBoard> component.
Consider adding an error boundary to your tree to customize error handling behavior.
```

## Root cause

`apps/desktop/src/App.tsx:152`:

```ts
const staleFlags = useStaleWorkDetection(state?.activeWork ?? {}, stalenessThresholdHours);
```

When `state` is `null` (initial render, OR no active project loaded), `state?.activeWork ?? {}` **allocates a fresh empty object `{}` on every render**.

`apps/desktop/src/hooks/useStaleWorkDetection.ts:87`:

```ts
useEffect(() => {
  const runDetection = () => {
    const nextFlags = computeStaleFlags(activeWork, thresholdHours);
    setStaleFlags(nextFlags); // fresh {} reference → state update → re-render
    // ...
  };
  runDetection();
  const id = setInterval(runDetection, 300_000);
  return () => clearInterval(id);
}, [activeWork, thresholdHours]); // activeWork dep is unstable
```

Cascade:

1. App renders, passes fresh `{}` to `useStaleWorkDetection`
2. Effect's `[activeWork, thresholdHours]` dep sees a new reference for `activeWork`
3. Effect re-runs → `runDetection()` → `setStaleFlags(computeStaleFlags({}, ...))` produces a fresh `{}` (line 23 of the hook)
4. `setStaleFlags` with a new reference → React schedules re-render
5. Re-render → fresh `{}` again → loop until `Maximum update depth exceeded` fires
6. `KanbanBoard` (downstream of the broken render cycle) throws
7. **No error boundary above the panel sections in `App.tsx`** → the throw unmounts the whole tree → blank WebView for users (production builds ship without `devtools` feature, so no console)

## Relationship to #210

Same bug class, different surface:

- #210 was about Zustand selectors returning `?? []` / `?? {}` inline. Fix: lift empty fallbacks to module-level constants (`EMPTY_TABS`, `EMPTY_COLUMN_ORDER`).
- This bug is one indirection out — the unstable allocation is at the **hook-args layer**, not inside a Zustand selector. The grep used to find #210 cases (`useStore.*?? []`) did not catch this pattern.

The "Consider adding an error boundary" warning in the console is the exact follow-up I flagged after #210 but did not file. Without an error boundary above the panel sections, every future analogous bug will produce the same blank-screen incident.

## Secondary suspect (less critical)

`App.tsx:357` (inside the file-changed listener) and `App.tsx:246` (inside `loadState`):

```ts
useTikiStateStore.getState().setRecentIssues(currentState?.history?.recentIssues || []);
```

`|| []` allocates fresh `[]` when `history.recentIssues` is missing. This fires on file watcher events (not every render), so it is not the loop driver. But it has the same shape and would cause downstream selector instability in subscribers of `useTikiStateStore.recentIssues`. Worth fixing in the same change.

## Proposed fix

Three edits in `apps/desktop/src/App.tsx`:

1. Add module-level stable constants near the top of the file (matching the v0.6.5 `EMPTY_TABS` / `EMPTY_COLUMN_ORDER` pattern):
   ```ts
   const EMPTY_ACTIVE_WORK: Record<string, WorkContext> = {};
   const EMPTY_RECENT_ISSUES: Array<{ number: number; title?: string; completedAt: string }> = [];
   ```
2. Replace `state?.activeWork ?? {}` at line 152 with `state?.activeWork ?? EMPTY_ACTIVE_WORK`.
3. Replace `currentState?.history?.recentIssues || []` at lines 246 and 357 with `currentState?.history?.recentIssues ?? EMPTY_RECENT_ISSUES`.

Plus the structural fix:

4. Add a React error boundary around the panel sections in App.tsx. Should wrap at least the center pane (KanbanBoard / DependencyGraph / TerminalPane / detail panels) so a single panel crash falls back to a recoverable error UI rather than unmounting the whole tree. The boundary should log the error (so a dev session sees it in console) and offer a "Reload" action.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `App.tsx` uses stable empty-constant fallbacks for `activeWork` and `recentIssues` (no fresh `{}` / `[]` allocations during render)
- [ ] Desktop dev session launches with no active project selected → KanbanBoard renders the empty-state UI without an infinite loop
- [ ] `Maximum update depth exceeded` does not appear in console during normal app lifecycle (open with project / open without project / switch projects)
- [ ] An error boundary is in place above the panel sections; deliberately throwing inside KanbanBoard (test via a one-line throw, then revert) shows a recoverable UI, not a blank tree
- [ ] grep-based or eslint check guards against re-introducing inline `?? {}` / `?? []` / `|| []` allocations in hook arguments AND useState/useEffect dep arrays (the #210 lesson, extended)

## Notes on workflow

Discovered while running `/tiki:execute` against issue #211 (which is about /tiki workflow Kanban transitions). Issue #211 is paused at Phase 2/4 (Phase 2 complete, uncommitted on disk); will resume after this v0.6.6 hotfix ships. v0.6.6 work is exclusively in `apps/desktop/src/` so it won't touch #211's `packages/framework/` changes.

lastCompletedIssue.title: Kanban pipeline doesn't reflect /tiki workflow transitions during execution

recentIssues.title: Kanban pipeline doesn't reflect /tiki workflow transitions during execution
Bulk YOLO: enqueue selected issues into a serial /tiki:yolo cascade
Batch operations follow-up: shift+click range, bulk remove-label, bulk YOLO, bulk assign-to-release
Batch operations on issues
Kanban card reordering within columns
Phase diff / change summary view
Auto-refresh sidebar lists (Issues / Releases / Pull Requests) after issue close, ship, or release completion
/tiki:release: run hands-off (no AskUserQuestion) and parallelize independent issues via worktree sub-agents
GitHub API rate limit handling
Pipeline Timeline: replace step numbers with step-specific icons (E55)
Skeleton loaders for issue list, plan fetch, and detail panel (E66)
Toast action buttons (Retry / Open Settings / etc.) (E68)
'Resume' / 'Send to terminal' button on Active Work cards (E7)
Real-time inline validation across IssueFormModal fields (E67)
Illustrated empty state for Kanban 'No issues' columns (E57)
Bug: Ctrl+C and Ctrl+V are completely non-functional in the terminal
Bug: Terminal clickable URLs not detected + Ctrl+V/Ctrl+C clipboard shortcuts not firing
Enhancement: 'Resume Conversation' action when the last command in a terminal was 'claude'
Enhancement: Add Settings explainer that Ctrl+Z in the terminal is SIGTSTP, not editor undo
Bug: PTY reader corrupts multi-byte UTF-8 across 4KB boundaries; also overloads IPC during Tab completion (E4)
Enhancement: Add 'Clear Scrollback' to terminal tab context menu + Ctrl+Shift+K shortcut (E1)
Bug: Ctrl+W and Ctrl+T in terminal close/open Tiki tabs instead of going to the shell
Bug: Ctrl+V double-pastes in terminal — custom keydown handler and xterm native paste event both write to PTY
Render 'Related:' soft references as dashed edges to distinguish from hard dependencies in the dep graph
Render GitHub labels as colored chips below the issue title in dep graph nodes
Animate dep graph edges only when work is actively flowing through them
Scale dep graph node height by plan phase count — small fixes look small, epics look big
Dep graph parser misses markdown 'Related:' sections — graph mostly empty across the project
Dependency Graph v2 — visual upgrade pack: swimlanes, in-node progress fills, hover-to-trace lineage
